小弟一一解答

The Economic Impacts of COVID-19
Coronavirus disease (COVID-19) is an infectious disease caused by a new virus. The coronavirus outbreak is a human tragedy. During this period, there is not enough medical staff in public hospital in Spain and Italy.
(a) An economist stated, `Although the government drastically increase the salary of the medical staff, the problem has been worsen.’ With the aid of diagram, justify his statement. (5 marks)
(b) A sector of the global economy that is feeling major pain from the coronavirus pandemic is the tourism industry. From tour guides to street vendors, everyone who depends on visitor spending is suffering losses. Also, many employees were laid off or request to work from home.
Discuss the effect of Coronavirus disease on economics variables include
(i) Aggregate Output
(ii) Unemployment rate
(iii) ONE other economics variable (12 marks)

自己作左條垃圾
歡迎玩下

replies.msg: 大致岩哂

some useful economics insights:

 The coronavirus (Covid-19) pandemic is challenge for economic policymakers.
 In most economic downturns, the appropriate policy response is qualitatively straightforward e.g. normal business cycle downturns usually represent a shortfall in AD for which the appropriate response is a cut in interest rates or perhaps a reduction in taxes and/or an increase in government spending.
 The Great Recession that followed the 2007-8 financial crisis was more challenging because it required not only aggressive policies to raise spending but also measures to repair and reinforce the financial system.
 This time is unusual, however, it is a direct consequence of the actions taken to deal with the health crisis and, in particular, to flatten out and delay the peak in infections so that health systems can cope
 But that has necessitated draconian limitations on people’s ability to go to work, school and shop. This reduce economic activity by somewhere between 25%-33%, for so long as they are in force.
 If the lockdowns are in effect for, say, three months that would knock something like 6-8 percentage points off annual GDP.
 That is a comfortably larger decline in GDP than during the Great Recession. And with the possibility of a second wave of infections occurring when restrictions are eased ?
 The impact on economic activity comes through several channels:
1) forced reductions in supply because of a reduced ability to operate effectively and disruption to supply chains;
and 2) forced reductions in demand, especially for many consumer goods and services.
 And, significantly, those D&S effects are almost entirely due to the disruptive effects of the lockdowns, not the direct consequences of people falling ill and being unable to work.

quote.msg:
The Economic Impacts of COVID-19
Coronavirus disease (COVID-19) is an infectious disease caused by a new virus. The coronavirus outbreak is a human tragedy. During this period, there is not enough medical staff in public hospital in Spain and Italy.
(a) An economist stated, `Although the government drastically increase the salary of the medical staff, the problem has been worsen.’ With the aid of diagram, justify his statement. (5 marks)
(b) A sector of the global economy that is feeling major pain from the coronavirus pandemic is the tourism industry. From tour guides to street vendors, everyone who depends on visitor spending is suffering losses. Also, many employees were laid off or request to work from home.
Discuss the effect of Coronavirus disease on economics variables include
(i) Aggregate Output
(ii) Unemployment rate
(iii) ONE other economics variable (12 marks)

自己作左條垃圾
歡迎玩下

replies.msg:
小弟今年dse試下答
(b)(i) As the number of tourists drops, export of services decreases. Aggregate demand and output drop. As many employees were laid off, the number of labour drops. Short-run aggregate supply and output drop.
(ii) Unemployment rate = (Unemploted population / labour force)
As the industry which depends on visitors is suffering losses, tour guides may be fired and lose their jobs. Unemployed population increases while labour force remains unchanged. Unemployment rate increases.
(iii) Economic variable: Fiscal balance
As the global economy suffers major pain from coronavirus, the companies worldwide have less profits and profit tax revenue decreases. The labours have less salaries due to the worsening economy, so profit tax revenue drops. At the same time, the government has to provide more unemployment benefits because of the increasing unemployment rate, so the govenment expenditure increases. The fiscal balance worsens as a result.
